title: add support for healthcheck on failure actions for 4.2.0

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *cdoern* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman-py). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman-py/blob/v4.2.0-rhel/OWNERS)~~ [cdoern] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

> Why was .cirrus.yml deleted?

cirrus does not have the images for podman-py going back this far, @cevich and I are going to figure out a solution for future releases
I think you were doing a point release for Jindrich, if so, please create one in the morning.

> cirrus does not have the images for podman-py going back this far,

It's worse than that - we're not monitoring CI on any branch other than main for this repo.  That will eventually lead to images being pruned beyond 30-days of inactivity.  This stance can change for future release-branches if needed, but there's nothing I can do to recover CI VM images beyond 60-days of last CI activity.
